title: ayenia integrifolia
intro: Ayenia integrifolia, also known as the whole-leaf ayenia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ayenia integrifolia include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, as well as potentially alleviating symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Ayenia integrifolia can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the plant's active compounds in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
